notes: Test 1 (settings-form): Both with and without skill produced nearly identical, high-quality output. The form pattern is well-known enough that the skill provides minimal incremental value for this common case.

Test 2 (warning-button-dialog): The skill correctly guided use of @base-ui/react Button primitive, render prop, data-slot attributes, ButtonGroup, and new button sizes (xs, icon-xs, etc.). Without the skill, the output used Radix patterns (asChild, Slot, forwardRef) and only had 4 size variants.
Test 3 (drawer-with-toast): The skill guided correct use of render prop on DrawerTrigger/DrawerClose and @phosphor-icons/react. Without skill, output used asChild (Radix) and lucide-react icons.

Key differentiator: The skill's primary value is enforcing Base UI patterns (render prop vs asChild) and project-specific conventions (@phosphor-icons, data-slot, oklch-based CSS variables). For standard React/shadcn patterns like forms, the baseline already performs well.
